revision.text:
[[ja:備前車塚古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|coordinates={{Coord|34.701667|N|133.976|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|architectural_styles={{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|qid=Q11397225|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun}}}} {{Infobox tumulus | name = Bizen Kurumazuka Kofun | image = [[File:Bizen Kurumazuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Kofun (Front section on the left, rear section in the back right) | alternative_name = Yubasa Kurumazuka Kofun | location = [[Okayama Prefecture]], {{ill|Okayama|en|Okayama|qid=Q200078|lt=Okayama|1=Okayama}}, {{ill|Naka-ku, Okayama|en|Naka-ku, Okayama|qid=Q852116|lt=Naka-ku|1=Naka-ku, Okayama}}, Shino-goze/Yubasa | latitude = 34.701667 | longitude = 133.976000 | type = [[Square-shaped anterior and posterior mound tumulus|Zenpō-Kōhō-fun]] (前方後方墳) | dimensions = Total length 48.3 m
Height 3 m (rear section) | burial = [[Pit-type stone chamber|Tateana-shiki sekishitsu]] (with an internal [[split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffin|Waritakegata-mokkan]]) | artifacts = 11 [[Sankakuen shinjukyo|Sankakuen shinjukyo]] mirrors, numerous other grave goods | built = Early [[Kofun period]] | burial_person = (One theory) [[Kibitsuhiko-no-Mikoto]] | historical_site = Not designated }} [[File:Bizen Kurumazuka Kofun, enkei.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Hill where the tumulus is located|qq=}}{{small|Located on the top of the central hill. The peak of Ryūnokuchi Mountain is at the end of the ridge extending left.|qq=}}]] '''Bizen Kurumazuka Kofun''' (備前車塚古墳, Bizen Kurumazuka Kofun), also known as '''Yubasa Kurumazuka Kofun''', is a [[Kofun]]-period Kofun located in '''{{ill|Naka-ku, Okayama|en|Naka-ku, Okayama|qid=Q852116|lt=Naka-ku|1=Naka-ku, Okayama}}, {{ill|Okayama|en|Okayama|qid=Q200078|lt=Okayama City|1=Okayama}}, Okayama Prefecture, Japan'''. It is classified as a '''[[Zenpō-kōhō-fun|Zenpō-Kōhō-fun]]''' (a tumulus with a square front and rear). It is not designated as a historic site. == Overview == This is an ancient tomb built along a ridge extending from {{ill|Ryūnokuchiyama|ja|龍ノ口山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryūnokuchiyama|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} to the east of the central urban area of Okayama City, in the southern part of Okayama Prefecture{{Sfn|Kurazuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988|qq=}}. The stone chamber was discovered in {{ill|1956|en|1956|qid=Q5221|lt=1956|1=1956}} ({{ill|Shōwa|en|Shōwa|qid=Q398996|lt=Shōwa|1=Shōwa}} 31), and grave goods were unearthed. Excavations were conducted in {{ill|1967|en|1967|qid=Q2648|lt=1967|1=1967}} and {{ill|1968|en|1968|qid=Q2644|lt=1968|1=1968}} ({{ill|Shōwa|en|Shōwa|qid=Q398996|lt=Shōwa|1=Shōwa}} 42 and 43) by the Kamaki Laboratory of {{ill|Okayama University of Science|en|Okayama University of Science|qid=Q3521222|lt=Okayama University of Science|1=Okayama University of Science}} (led by Kamaki Yoshimasa) and the Archaeology Laboratory of {{ill|Okayama University|en|Okayama University|qid=Q1067916|lt=Okayama University|1=Okayama University}} (led by {{ill|Kondō Yoshirō|ja|近藤義郎|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kondō Yoshirō|1=Yoshirō Kondō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11638900}}){{Sfn|Bizen Kurazuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989|qq=}}. The mound has a front-rear rectangular shape, with the front section widening in a fan shape, facing west. The mound is built in two tiers. No {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|qid=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}} have been found on the surface, but it clearly retains a stone covering ({{ill|Fukiishi|en|Fukiishi|qid=Q11621213|lt=Fukiishi|1=Fukiishi}}) arranged in rows with angular pebbles{{Sfn|Kurazuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1988|qq=}}{{Sfn|Bizen Kurazuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989|qq=}}. The burial facility consists of a {{ill|Tateana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tateana-shiki sekishitsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} (pit-style stone chamber) located at the center of the rear section, excavated into the natural bedrock{{Sfn|Bizen Kurazuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989|qq=}}. The stone chamber's main axis is perpendicular to the mound's main axis and measures 5.9 meters in length, 1.2 meters in width, and approximately 1.5 meters in height. The top of the stone chamber is covered with eight ceiling stones, and inside, a {{ill|Waritakegata mokkan|ja|割竹形木棺|1=Waritakegata Mokkan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Waritakegata mokkan|13=User:Immanuelle/Waritakegata Mokkan|qq=}} (split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffin) is placed on a clay floor{{Sfn|Bizen Kurazuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989|qq=}}. Numerous grave goods were discovered inside the stone chamber, including 11 {{ill|Sankakuen shinjūkyō|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sankakuen shinjūkyō|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=}} (triangular-rimmed deity-and-beast mirrors). {{ill|Bizen Kurazuka Kofun|ja|備前車塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bizen Kurazuka Kofun|qq=}} is estimated to have been built in the early {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|qid=Q459939|lt=Kofun period|1=Kofun period}}. The abundance of grave goods suggests the significant power of the ruling entity at the time, and it is also considered an important reference for studies on the casting of triangular-rimmed deity-and-beast mirrors. Although the identity of the buried individual remains unknown, one theory, as mentioned in the *Okayama City History*, identifies the tomb with {{ill|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|en|Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|qid=Q65318|lt=Ōkibitsuhiko no Mikoto|1=Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto}}, an ancestor of the {{ill|Kibi clan|ja|吉備上道臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibi clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} clan mentioned in the *{{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|qid=Q813031|lt=Kojiki|1=Kojiki}}**Okayama City History, Ancient Section*, Okayama City, 1962 (based on an explanation board at the historical site).. == Mound Structure == The '''dimensions of the tumulus''' are as follows:[http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/05.html Bizen Kurumazuka Kofun] - Okayama City Museum. * '''Total length''': 48.3 m * '''Rear section''' (two-tiered) ''' Length: 23 m ''' Height: 3 m * '''Front section''' (trapezoidal, two-tiered) ''' Length: 21.8 m File:Bizen Kurumazuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|Rear section summit File:Bizen Kurumadzuka Kofun 03.jpg|Fuki-ishi (cover stones) of the rear section File:Bizen Kurumazuka Kofun, kouhoubu.jpg|View from the front section toward the rear File:Bizen Kurumazuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear section toward the front File:Bizen Kurumazuka Kofun, kubirebu.jpg|Narrowed section (central rear, with cover stones extending perpendicularly) == Burial Artifacts == Artifacts recovered from the '''1956 excavation''' include: * '''Bronze mirrors''' (13 total, all '''Chinese imports''') ''' '''11 Sankakuen shinjukyo mirrors''' '''* 8 types, 9 mirrors (two are identical) '''* '''Identical to mirrors found at [[Tsubai Ōtsukayama Kofun]] (Kyoto) and sites from Kyushu to North Kanto'''. ''' '''1 Naigai-kamonkyō (内行花文鏡)''' (mirrors with inner floral patterns) ''' '''1 Gakumondai shinjukyo (画文帯神獣鏡)''' (mirrors with pictorial god-and-beast motifs) * '''Iron weapons and tools''' ''' '''Iron sword (Tachi)''' ''' '''Iron dagger (Ken)''' ''' '''Iron spear or halberd''' ''' '''Iron arrowheads (7)''' ''' '''Iron axe''' ''' '''Iron short-handled axe''' Additional artifacts from the '''1967–1968 excavation''' included '''iron arrowheads, iron axes, iron swords, iron rods, chisels, and quivers'''. The recovered '''mirrors are preserved at {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|en|Tokyo National Museum|qid=Q653433|lt=Tokyo National Museum|1=Tokyo National Museum}}'''.Historical marker at the site. File:備前車塚古墳出土 三角縁獣文帯五神四獣鏡 (複製).JPG|Replica of a '''Sankakuen shinjukyo mirror'''
{{small|{{ill|Tokyo National Museum|en|Tokyo National Museum|qid=Q653433|lt=Tokyo National Museum|1=Tokyo National Museum}} exhibition.|qq=}} File:備前車塚古墳出土 三角縁陳氏作四神二獣鏡 (複製).JPG|Replica of a '''Sankakuen Chinshi-make shijin nishukyo mirror'''
{{small|Tokyo National Museum exhibition.|qq=}} File:備前車塚古墳出土 三角縁二神六獣鏡 (複製).JPG|Replica of a '''Sankakuen nigami rokushukyo mirror'''
{{small|Tokyo National Museum exhibition.|qq=}} == Related Sites == * '''{{ill|Tokyo National Museum|en|Tokyo National Museum|qid=Q653433|lt=Tokyo National Museum|1=Tokyo National Museum}}''' (Tokyo) - Houses artifacts from Bizen Kurumazuka Kofun. == References == {{reflist|qq=}} == Bibliography == * Historical marker at the site. * {{Cite book|author=Yoshimasa Kamaki|title=Kurumazuka Kofun|publisher=Kokushi Daijiten|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Kurumazuka (Kokushi)|qq=}}|qq=}} * {{Cite book|author=Saburō Kobayashi|title=Kofun Encyclopedia|publisher=Tokyo-do Publishing|year=1989|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Bizen Kurumazuka Kofun (Kofun)|qq=}}|qq=}} * {{Cite book|author=Makoto Norioka, Hiromi Gyoda|title=The Kofun of Kibi|publisher=Kibito Publishing|year=2000|isbn=490657744X|pages=62-65|ref=|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/05.html Bizen Kurumazuka Kofun] - Okayama City Museum. {{Kofun navbar|qq=}} {{translated page|ja|備前車塚古墳|version=101786606|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:備前車塚古墳]] [[zh:三角縁神獣鏡が確認された主な古墳]] [[ja:岡山市]] [[ja:鶴山丸山古墳]] [[ja:備前市]] [[ja:白鳥古墳]] [[ja:東広島市]] [[ja:潮崎山古墳]] [[ja:福山市]] [[ja:中小田1号墳]] [[ja:広島市]] [[ja:神原神社古墳]] [[ja:雲南市]] [[ja:竹島御家老屋敷古墳]] [[ja:周南市]] [[ja:奥三号墳]] [[ja:さぬき市]] [[ja:西山古墳]] [[ja:宮谷古墳]] [[ja:徳島市]] [[ja:那珂八幡古墳]] [[ja:福岡市]] [[ja:石塚山古墳]] [[ja:京都郡]] [[ja:一貴山銚子塚古墳]] [[ja:糸島市]] [[ja:岩上祭祀遺構]] [[ja:宗像市]] [[ja:赤塚古墳]] [[ja:宇佐市]] [[ja:川部・高森古墳群]] [[ja:持田古墳群]] [[ja:児湯郡]] [[ja:高鍋町]] {{Commons category|Bizen Kurumazuka Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q11387347}} [[Category:Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] [[Category:History of Naka Ward, Okayama City]] [[Category:岡山県の古墳]]
